checks.finding: REQ-001..011 each specify measurable behavior (bundling, tool/resource counts, golden-test equivalence, allowlist/denylist enforcement, CI matrix, installer flags) with corresponding AC-001..017 evidence targets in acceptance-tests.md.

requirements.md Acceptance Criteria section and acceptance-tests.md Requirement column jointly map every REQ-001..011 to at least one AC-001..017 (e.g., REQ-005->AC-001/AC-002, REQ-006->AC-003/AC-004, REQ-009->AC-007/AC-008/AC-009, REQ-011->AC-012).

Each AC in acceptance-tests.md names a concrete observable outcome: exact error codes (not-found, cannot-parse, not-sdd-root, too-large), exit-code/message equivalence with check-task-state.sh, schema conformance, file mtime invariance, and install/uninstall file-presence checks.

Non-goals section explicitly excludes knowledge-mcp/local-env-mcp, all write tools (Approval changes, SDD_SUDO, tasks.md transitions), Cursor/VS Code connection work, CI artifact retrieval via gh, npm registry publish, and evidence signature verification execution.

Round-3 edit resolves the prior round-2 contradiction: Edge Cases now states the file size limit as a fixed 2 MiB value ('design.md Data Plan と同値') with the too-large error code named, matching AC-017's '2 MiB' threshold verbatim, so the constraint is a spec-approved constant rather than a design-deferred parameter. Path allowlist/denylist, root-fixation precedence (REQ-007), Node>=20, and bash 3.2 compatibility are also explicit.

The Risks section's high-risk claim (parser misjudgment causing quality-gate false pass) has an explicit validation surface: golden-test equivalence against check-task-state.sh (REQ-005, AC-001, TEST-001) enforced in CI per acceptance-tests.md Notes.
